question.text: Under Section 23JA of SCRA, what is the settlement mechanism for defaults?

answer.text: The defaulter can apply in writing to SEBI proposing settlement, and SEBI may agree considering nature, gravity and impact of defaults; no appeal lies against settlement orders
Settlement is only possible through SAT mediation
Only criminal courts can approve settlement of SCRA violations
Settlement is automatic upon payment of the maximum penalty amount

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 23JA allows a defaulter to apply in writing to SEBI proposing settlement. SEBI, considering the nature, gravity, and impact of defaults, may agree on payment of such sum or other terms. Importantly, no appeal lies against settlement orders, and all settlement amounts (excluding disgorgement and legal costs) go to the Consolidated Fund of India. **The Trap:** Settlement is not automatic — SEBI has discretion. Settlement amounts go to CFI (not SEBI's IPEF). No appeal against consent orders ensures finality. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Settlement under SCRA goes to the Consolidated Fund, while disgorgement under SEBI Act goes to IPEF — note this distinction for exam purposes.
